notes: Single source of truth for candidate venues. The script reads THIS file to decide what to query (weather + flights). 'travel' gives how each person reaches the venue: mode fly|local|drive and the destination airport IATA they fly into (Michel from London, Dan from Belfast).

venues.name: Fair Head, NI
venues.why: Dan lives in Belfast — cheapest logistics; world-class venue.

venues.name: Mournes, NI
venues.why: In climbing-trips.csv: Moderate, ~150m, Full Range, <4h, 2-day min. Local to Dan.

venues.name: Dolomites (Cortina)
venues.why: Confirmed backup if NI is wet. Peak season late July; routes already scoped in dolomites-trip.csv.

venues.name: East Tyrol (Lienz)
venues.why: Second alpine alternative with a similar settled summer window.

venues.name: Lake District (Borrowdale)
venues.why: Cheap UK backup, drivable for Michel; good when a dry spell hits Cumbria. In climbing-trips.csv.

venues.name: Snowdonia (Llanberis Pass)
venues.why: Classic UK mountain trad (Dinas Cromlech etc.); drivable, cheap. In climbing-trips.csv as Llanberis.

venues.name: Isle of Arran
venues.why: Granite ridges/slabs; reachable via Glasgow + ferry. In climbing-trips.csv as Aaran.

venues.name: Picos de Europa
venues.why: Cooler N. Spain alpine rock — better than baking S. Spain in late July. In climbing-trips.csv.

venues.name: Paklenica (Anica Kuk)
venues.why: In the Velebit mountains, not too hot in July IF you pick shade — most crags face E/W and Anica Kuk faces NORTH (stays shaded); sea nearby to cool off. Season Apr–Oct. Sources: climbgrades.com/seasons/croatia, mojagear.com Paklenica guide.
